text_source: Ahh... phew.
With a satisfied yawn, I shut the children's\n
book in my lap.
That's the last of the books Nekone\n
recommended...
She brought them at Kuon's request. They're all\n
like this--picture books for children.
It's not like I hate reading. Each one of these\n
tales and myths is engaging, but...\n
unsophisticated.
Since these books are meant for just learning\n
the letters, I guess that's to be expected,\n
but...
At least I can read something like this without\n
any trouble, now.
I won't have to listen to that little twerp\n
asking why I can't read in that baffled tone\n
any more.
I wouldn't have figured before she asked me\n
that words without malice can hurt all the\n
same.
Yo, kid. Got a moment?
Ukon appears in the doorway.
...Sorry to intrude.
Behind Ukon trails a boy of small, slight\n
stature.
Hello. I'm sorry for skipping out the other day\n
before we could be introduced properly.
Ah, this guy... Kiwru, I think? Nekone called\n
him Oshtor's sworn brother.
I've got a request from Lord Oshtor for you.\n
Something he's been meaning to bring up.
What do you mean, "Lord Oshtor?"\n
Why's the man himself referring t--
Ukon's smirk cuts my thoughts short.
He... hasn't told Kiwru his true identity,\n
has he?
Kiwru fidgets, looking puzzled and uncomfortable.\n
He doesn't seem to know why he's even here.
Good grief. He really doesn't have a clue.
As Oshtor, he's the definition of straight-laced.\n
So why is his Ukon identity so...?
This might be a little fun. Let's keep going.
I grin back at him.
So, this is the message for you from\n
Lord Oshtor.
He says this kid's gonna be doing work for you\n
so he can get some proper experience.
Huh!?
Oh, so we're taking him on as a colleague.
That's right. Comes from the big man himself,\n
so do what you please with him. Put the kid\n
to work.
W-Wait, I haven't heard anything about that!
Of course you haven't. I didn't bring it up\n
'til just now.
It can't be...
Well, let me finish, here. You'll change your\n
tune if you listen through to the end.
Urgh...
Surprised, Kiwru hesitantly begins to stand,\n
but sits back down at Ukon's placations.
This is all Oshtor's plan to begin with, so I\n
don't mind, but... is this kid gonna be able\n
to keep up?
The way Lord Oshtor tells it, he's never once\n
missed a session of his military arts training.\n
He's good.
Oh?
I wouldn't have guessed it by his gentle\n
appearance, but if he has Ukon's vote, there's\n
no mistake.
I'll be glad for some more male company around\n
here, honestly. He doesn't seem unpleasant.
Word from our Missy's that you want to keep a\n
small, exceptional pool of talent on call for\n
stuff.
Yeah, something like that.
Not that I had any choice in the matter, what\n
with Kuon blowing all our money on paying for\n
the inn.
If that's the case, then look no further.\n
I figured you could use a guy with some chops--\n
here he is.
Hold on, um... Ukon, was it?
You said you were... an agent of my honored\n
brother. Is that really true?
You appeared uninvited, brought me here, roped\n
me into this--and he never mentioned a word of\n
it.
I'd hoped to become my brother's assistant,\n
like Nekone. There must be some kind of\n
misunderstanding...
O-Or else you're deciding these things on your\n
own, which I doubt.
Ah, well, about that. It's not a\n
misunderstanding.
This guy and I, we're under direct orders from\n
Oshtor, and this is how he wants things to go\n
down.
He said no matter how hard he tries, he's\n
always too lenient on his nearest relations...
And that becoming a full-fledged warrior means\n
overcoming hardships and gaining real-world\n
experience.
But if you insist, I can have a talk with him\n
and see about putting you somewhere else,\n
maybe?
That's...
I definitely... want to be a full-fledged\n
warrior, but... if I do this, I won't... be\n
with Nekone...
And now he's started to fret with his head in\n
his hands. Easy to see the inner conflict,\n
though.
But if he believes someone he just met this\n
easily... Maybe we've gotta work on that\n
naivete.
We're back!
We have returned.
While Kiwru holds his head in conflicted agony,\n
Kuon and Nekone return from their shopping trip.
N-Nekone!
Oh, hello. What are you doing here? With my\n
dear brother, no less...
Why? I was brought here all of a sudden,\n
and this--\n
...Dear brother?
Ah, cripes.
Kiwru looks between Nekone and Ukon several\n
times.
Huh? But that's--you--um? I... bwuh? What do\n
you mean, "dear brother"?
I don't think I could ever get bored of\n
watching his facial expressions change that\n
rapidly.
Dear brother, what is this?
C'mon, don't call him "this." He's a respected\n
prince back home in Ennakamuy.
I've told you this before. It's cruel not to\n
treat him with the proper respect.
...Not that you're being rude or anything\n
yourself.
I-I-I... Are you really... my honorable\n
brother...?
Ah... Bahahaha! Yeah, I guess the cat's out of\n
the bag, huh. It's me, all right. Oshtor! Your\n
brother.
Hey. What's wrong?
I might remind you, brother, that Kiwru has\n
all the strength of heart of a sparrow.\n
Hold your jests.
I... see. So that's why you're dressed like\n
that.
Yeah. It's just a disguise to escape public\n
notice.
But... Y-Your personality is completely\n
different, too...
What are you saying? It wouldn't be a disguise\n
if it were totally the same.
That's... true, but...
Getting disillusioned?
Huh? N-No, not at all--
I was disillusioned.
N-Nekone!?
The day I first arrived in the imperial capital,\n
I thought to rush immediately to my brother.
Upon finding him, I was greeted by the sight of\n
my dear sibling drunk and dancing naked among\n
other men.
The disillusionment I felt then will stay with\n
me for the rest of my life.
Huh...
Ahahaha! We'd all had a little bit to drink,\n
I'll admit.
I can see the life draining from Kiwru's eyes\n
as he stares off into space vacantly.
Mm, I think it's just about time. Rulutieh,\n
Nekone. Help me get some tea ready.
OK.
Yes, dear sister.
Oh... P-Please, do not trouble yourself with...
Don't worry about all that. I'm making tea\n
because I want to, so you shouldn't be so\n
modest.
We just bought some delicious-looking teacakes!\n
Help yourself to those, if you like.
Sister, should we use... this tea, perhaps?
Mhm. We have guests, so we should go all-out and\n
use the best we have.
Besides, it's not like we can use it when there\n
isn't a special occasion for it.
Nekone smiles as Kuon speaks--as though spurred\n
on by her words.
That Nekone. She was reluctant when I sent\n
her out here, y'know, but now she's totally\n
attached.
Makes a guy feel a little lonely, to be honest.
I think she'll come back to you again when you\n
stop dressing like that and go back to your\n
old self.
...I'm just glad she's doing well.
You really prefer this appearance over the\n
other one, don't you?
H-Huh? By any chance... Does Nekone work with\n
this group as well?
Yeah, Uk--I mean Osh--geez, this is confusing.\n
Basically, he sent her to keep us from being\n
shorthanded.
She runs her mouth and she's a little imp,\n
but she's a competent imp, which makes it even\n
worse.
What was that?
He's talking about how I sent you to work with\n
the kid.
Oh, about that.
I asked him what we would do if something awful\n
befell me, and he said...
...it was better than attracting some sleazebag\n
off the street. I feel rather like a live\n
sacrifice.
Huh? H-Huh!?
Oh, stop saying stuff like that.\n
I haven't laid a finger on you.
For now.
...Is he getting that vacant stare again?
Incidentally, why are you and Kiwru even here?
Ah, well. I was gonna have Kiwru help out\n
around here, but he doesn't seem too keen on\n
Huh?
If he doesn't want to, I guess it can't be\n
helped. I can put him back in my office and--
I'll do it, I'll do it!\n
I'll do my best here!
Oh? I'm grateful to hear you say that, but\n
don't force yourself on my account.
No, it's fine! I can do it! I'll do my best!
Y-Yeesh, all right. If you insist so much, I'm\n
hardly complaining. It'd be a big help to me.
I'll be countin' on you.
Yessir!
He'll be under our supervision, then... We're\n
still shorthanded, but this is definitely a\n
step.
Then we'll be comrades from now on, seems like.\n
I look forward to working with you.
Yes, me too!
Kiwru's joining up with us, hm? It's a pleasure\n
to be working with you.
Yes, it's a pleasure to be working with you\n
as well...
I see. Kiwru is to be one of us.
I-I look forward to working with you, Nekone.\n
And you too, Rulutieh!
I-I look forward to working with you...
And I as well.
O--OK!
I already kinda had it figured out, but man,\n
he responded to her way differently than he\n
did to me.
If that's all settled, then, let's dispense with\n
the serious topics. The tea's getting cold.
Here, Lord Kiwru. You have some too.
Thank you. And please--you don't have to be\n
so formal.
My standing and titles don't mean anything\n
here. I'm just a novice.
You make for a natural underling either way,\n
really.
Aha... ahaha...
Do try not to cause trouble for anyone while\n
you are here.
Does Nekone just... hate me...?
Kiwru's face falls as he mutters to himself.
Don't worry about it. She's like that with\n
everybody.
Nekone, do you have a moment?
What is it, dear sister?
Your hair's coming undone. Hold still so I can\n
fix it up, all right?
Ah...
I'll help, Miss Kuon...
OK, you do that side, Rulutieh.
Your beautiful hair, all disheveled like this...\n
The wind must have pulled it loose.
Well, the wind was strong today, so...
Between them, Kuon and Rulutieh untie Nekone's\n
hair and carefully begin to comb it out.
Nekone squints, then seems to relax comfortably,\n
yielding to their attentions.
...I've never seen Nekone like that...
It's an exception. Doesn't count.
She treats a lot of people the way she treats\n
you. I don't think she specifically hates you\n
or anything.
Yeah, you're the same as the rest.\n
Don't worry about it so much.
Ahaha... ha...
That's odd. This is supposed to be a sweet\n
snack, but it tastes... salty...?
